nuggets.headline: Facing challenges as a Queer Black Woman in the South led Jamee Cornelia to 'travel OFTEN'
nuggets.text: Jamee Cornelia identifies as a Queer Black Woman, and she's been open about facing significant challenges and not being taken seriously in the male-dominated creative scene of the South, particularly in Cobb County and her North Carolina hometown. She's found a place to truly thrive in East Atlanta, and her powerful advice to anyone facing similar hurdles is to 'travel OFTEN,' believing in the strength and self-worth found in exploring new environments.

source.title: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
source.quoteSnippet: Jamee Cornelia is a Queer Black Woman...faced challenges and was not taken seriously due to her youth, gender, race, and sexual orientation...her advice to women or anyone experiencing similar feelings is to 'travel OFTEN'

nuggets.imageCaption: Jamee Cornelia, a Queer Black Woman artist from North Carolina.

nuggets.headline: The name 'L7 Records' connects Jamee Cornelia's latest album, 'HARVEST', to her high school dreams
nuggets.text: The album 'HARVEST', which features 'SLACK', is Jamee Cornelia's latest full-length release, showcasing 13 new songs. It arrives courtesy of L7 Records, the indie label she co-runs with friends from her Visibly Inflight collective. The label's name itself is a nod to her roots, stemming from a clothing store she dreamed of opening in high school for her very first creative venture: a clothing company.

source.title: ‎Jamee Cornelia - Apple Music
source.quoteSnippet: HARVEST (2026), 13 songs...She co-runs an indie label called "L7 Records" with friends from the Visibly Inflight collective. L7 Records is named after a store she wanted to open for her clothing company in high school at Marietta Square.

nuggets.headline: For another artist with laid-back grooves and clever bars, check out Nappy Nina
nuggets.text: If the smooth flow and intricate production of Jamee Cornelia on 'SLACK' have you hooked, you absolutely need to dive into the world of Nappy Nina. Her music often features a similar unhurried, yet deeply engaging vocal delivery over beats that are both fresh and sometimes wonderfully off-kilter. You'll find yourself appreciating the thoughtful lyricism and chill vibes, just like with Jamee's work.
nuggets.imageCaption: Nappy Nina, an artist sharing sonic qualities with Jamee Cornelia.

artistSummary: Jamee Cornelia is a Queer Black Woman from Fuquay Varina, North Carolina, who built her creative career from the ground up, starting with a clothing company at 15. She's a versatile artist, balancing solo music, leading a punk band, being part of a hip-hop collective, directing music videos, and co-running an indie label.

externalLinks.label: Conversations with the Inspiring Jamee Cornelia
